note: T9 golden set for the Pilot router (spec §6.4, floor: Pilot final-decision accuracy >= 0.80). Rows are self-authored, rubric-anchored (scripts/pilot_router/rubric.md v1) prompts chosen to be unambiguous exemplars of each tier, including code-heavy turns (spec risk #3). No rows are drawn from the held-out test split or any labeled corpus turn. Some prompts are stylistically inspired by public WildChat-1M user turns (Zhao et al., ODC-BY 1.0, https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/WildChat-1M) but are original text authored for this file, not copied. Gold labels assigned per the labeling rubric; ties resolved DOWN per rule 5. The first line is this metadata header and is skipped by the CI test.

review this Dockerfile for correctness and layer-caching efficiency and suggest concrete improvements: FROM python:3.12
COPY . /app
RUN pip install -r /app/requirements.txt
CMD python /app/main.py
convert this recursive fibonacci to an iterative version and explain why the recursive one is slow: def fib(n): return n if n<2 else fib(n-1)+fib(n-2)
write a short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers a message in a bottle
translate this couplet into English while preserving its meter and rhyme: Le vent se lève, il faut tenter de vivre; / L'air immense ouvre et referme mon livre.

    task = asyncio.sleep(1)
    print('done')
asyncio.run(main())
given a table orders(user_id, created_at, status) with frequent queries filtering user_id and status and sorting by created_at, which composite index would you create and why?

explain why no consensus protocol can guarantee both safety and liveness in an asynchronous network with one faulty process, and sketch the core argument of the FLP result

architect a distributed job scheduler that guarantees exactly-once execution across data centers despite clock skew and network partitions, and justify how you prevent duplicate runs
propose a concrete architecture and 6-month incremental plan to make our untested 200k-line monolith testable without a big rewrite, with milestones and risk mitigation

design a concurrent, low-pause garbage collector for a managed runtime, describe the write barrier and tri-color invariant, and argue why it never collects a live object
